name: Los Chorradores — Circular from Navarrés
region_intro: Hiking around Navarrés offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by natural pools, waterfalls, and lush riverine vegetation within the La Canal de Navarrés region. The terrain includes well-marked trails leading through areas shaped by water, providing varied elevation changes suitable for different hiking abilities. This area is known for its unique geological formations and natural water features, making it a compelling destination for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Navarrés The most popular hiking route is Los Chorradores — Circular from Navarrés, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route explores the renowned Los Chorradores Municipal Natural Park, featuring waterfalls and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Circular walk through the Charcos de Quesa, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail leads through a series of natural pools carved into rock, offering distinct scenery. Local hikers also love the Bolbaite natural pool – El Salto de Chella Viewpoint loop from Bolbaite, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through natural pools and to a waterfall viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Navarrés is defined by captivating waterfalls, natural pools, and diverse river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging paths. The routes in Navarrés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 280 reviews. More than 1,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Navarrés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navarrés?
A: There are over 75 hiking routes in the Navarrés area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging paths through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Navarrés?
A: Yes, Navarrés offers several family-friendly hiking options. The region is known for its accessible trails, especially those leading to natural pools and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818174273/los-chorradores-circular-from-navarres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chorradores — Circular from Navarrés&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, explores a natural park with amenities like information panels and rest areas, making it suitable for families.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Navarrés?
A: Many of the trails around Navarrés are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818174273/los-chorradores-circular-from-navarres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chorradores — Circular from Navarrés&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the stunning natural park. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818068753/circular-walk-through-the-charcos-de-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk through the Charcos de Quesa&lt;/a&gt;, exploring a series of natural pools.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on a hike in Navarrés?
A: Navarrés is rich in natural beauty, particularly known for its water features. You can expect to see captivating waterfalls, natural pools, and lush riverine vegetation. Highlights include the spectacular Los Chorradores double waterfall, the warm mineral waters of Pozo de las Quebradas, and the refreshing Playamonte Lagoon. You might also encounter ancient rock art sites, recognized as UNESCO World Heritage Sites, in the wider region.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely! Waterfalls are a major attraction in Navarrés. The Los Chorradores Municipal Natural Park is famous for its captivating waterfalls, including the impressive El Chorrador. Other notable waterfalls in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaspar Waterfall and Pool&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Navarrés?
A: The hiking trails around Navarrés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 280 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the area's stunning natural pools and waterfalls, the diverse terrain, and the well-marked paths that make exploring accessible and enjoyable.

Q: Are there any natural swimming spots along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Navarrés is renowned for its natural swimming spots. The Los Chorradores Municipal Natural Park features deep blue swimming holes at the base of its waterfalls. Additionally, the Pozo de las Quebradas offers warm, mineral-rich waters, and the Playamonte Lagoon provides an inland beach experience perfect for a refreshing dip after a hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818068753/circular-walk-through-the-charcos-de-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk through the Charcos de Quesa&lt;/a&gt; also leads to a series of natural pools.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navarrés?
A: The best time to hike in Navarrés is generally during spring and autumn when the temperatures are milder and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Spring brings lush greenery and full waterfalls, while autumn offers pleasant weather for exploration. Summer can be warm, but the numerous natural pools provide excellent opportunities for cooling off.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Navarrés is home to ancient rock art sites, such as those found at El Garrofero. These UNESCO World Heritage Sites can be visited through guided activities, often integrated with local trails. You might also encounter historical remnants like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4027751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla Tambuc&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hikes in Navarrés?
A: Navarrés offers a range of difficulty levels to suit various hikers. Out of 76 routes, 25 are considered easy, 46 are moderate, and 5 are more difficult. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced hikers can find suitable trails, from gentle walks to more challenging paths with varied elevation changes.

Q: Are there any caves or gorges to explore near Navarrés?
A: Yes, the region around Navarrés features interesting geological formations including caves and gorges. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4063316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cabrenta Cave&lt;/a&gt; and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; gorge, which offers dramatic scenery and unique natural structures.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Navarrés?
A: Many popular hiking areas in Navarrés, such as the Los Chorradores Municipal Natural Park, offer designated parking facilities. It's generally advisable to check specific trailhead information or local signage upon arrival, but accessibility for vehicles is typically good for the main attractions.

The Chorrador de Navarrés, also known as Los Chorradores, is one of the most spectacular natural sites in the interior of the Valencian Community, ideal for a refreshing getaway in contact with nature.
